vulnerabilities.type: Remote Code Execution (Werkzeug Debug Console exposed)
vulnerabilities.description: Werkzeug debugger console is publicly accessible (SECRET revealed, PIN-protected). If the PIN is obtained from server logs/environment, it enables arbitrary Python code execution.
Introspection is enabled, exposing the schema (Query, UserType incl. 'flag'), allowing attackers to enumerate operations and sensitive fields.

Endpoint accepts stringified JSON filters (search/options) from the client. Unauthenticated clients can pass operators like $regex and set is_admin: true to enumerate admin users, indicating NoSQL-style injection/broken access control.

vulnerabilities.type: Broken access control / Unauthenticated data exposure
vulnerabilities.description: The users query is publicly accessible and accepts parameters (search, isAdmin) enabling retrieval of admin and non-admin user data without authentication.
UserType exposes a 'flag' field that can be queried, indicating overexposed internal/sensitive fields even if currently null/empty.
